article_title: Hair-Thin Nerve Implants Decode Phantom Leg Movements in Above-Knee Amputees, Opening Path to Neurally Controlled Prosthetics

findings.message: This article was requested by a human editor — apply heightened scrutiny to content accuracy and source quality

content_preview.title: Hair-Thin Nerve Implants Decode Phantom Leg Movements in Above-Knee Amputees, Opening Path to Neurally Controlled Prosthetics
content_preview.summary: Ultrathin electrodes in the sciatic nerve and a spiking neural network decoder classify phantom leg movements in above-knee amputees, enabling bidirectional motor control and sensory feedback from one device.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A team led by Chalmers University of Technology in Sweden has demonstrated, for the first time, the decoding of intended leg movements directly from peripheral nerves in people with above-knee amputations. The proof-of-concept study, [published in Nature Communications](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-026-69297-0), used hair-thin electrodes implanted in the sciatic nerve and a bio-inspired artificial intelligence decoder to classify phantom movements of the knee, ankle, and t...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent biomedical research article with exceptional technical depth. Specific numerical claims all properly sourced. Clear explanation of SNN decoder advantages.
editor_notes.source_verification: 3 sources checked. PMC/Nature Communications paper: VERIFIED — confirms 2 participants, TIME electrodes, 56 channels at 30 kHz, 91% vs 37.5% responsiveness, SNN accuracy (58.3%/68.3%, combined 64.3%/72.6%), Pearson 0.93, 7% motor-sensory overlap, 90-day period, foreign body response. EurekAlert: VERIFIED — confirms Giacomo Valle, Chalmers, bidirectional capability, SNN approach, FeelAgain grant. Nature.com not separately fetched (same paper as PMC). Human-requested article reviewed with heightened scrutiny.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All numerical claims verified against the peer-reviewed paper. Decoder accuracy figures, electrode specifications, responsiveness rates, and motor-sensory overlap statistics all match exactly.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and scientifically precise. Appropriately notes limitations without sensationalizing the breakthrough.
editor_notes.originality: No prior neuroprosthetics coverage. New topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Outstanding human-requested biomedical article. Peer-reviewed source validates all claims. Approved.
